source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

claim_chain: Affective signals influence motor control through parallel cortico-subcortical circuits
Anatomical evidence suggests PUTv-centered open-loop circuit may operate alongside canonical sensorimotor circuit
Whether these circuits are functionally independent and differentially recruited by incentive conditions remains unknown
Connectomic data reveal PUTv maintains balanced connectivity with affective and motor networks
Task fMRI shows jackpot and robber incentives produce distinct behavioral and neural signatures
High reward downregulates CLC while maintaining OLC engagement
High loss avoidance eliminates both circuits and recruits stopping-related regions
Findings support versatile architecture that flexibly engages distinct circuits depending on incentive phenomenology

typical_pattern: State finding or observation from data
Connect to anatomical or functional evidence from literature
Link to broader theoretical framework or next steps

likely_priorities: Direct demonstration of functional pathways in primates
Characterization of CLC and OLC configurations involving CMA and basal ganglia in primates
Mechanistic validation of proposed multilevel control architecture
Causal evidence linking PUTv-CMA pathway to movement initiation
likely_attacks: Functional connectivity does not establish causal pathways
Partialling approach may not fully isolate independent PUTv contributions
Task design confounds incentive valence with magnitude
Speed-accuracy trade-off interpretation requires more trials or conditions
Proposed model awaits direct demonstration and primate validation
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly states 'Direct demonstration of this functional pathway awaits further investigation' and 'CLC and OLC configurations involving CMA and basal ganglia remain to be characterized in primates.' The discussion acknowledges that connectivity data 'suggest' rather than prove functional independence, and that interpretations are 'parsimonious' or 'plausible' rather than definitive. The paper also notes that the speed-accuracy trade-off occurs 'at least in early trials,' suggesting awareness of temporal limitations. These hedges and caveats indicate the authors anticipate reviewers will want causal validation, primate data, and more comprehensive task designs.

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers were included. The profile accurately reflects the paper's cautious-but-assertive tone, mechanism-gap opening, and balanced claim strength with appropriate hedging.
